page_content:
Admission to the Major

Students will meet with an advisor to review the requirements for the major and create a four-year plan. Advisors engage with students to help them select courses that appeal to their current interests, while introducing them to new and challenging areas of study. Students pursuing the language-enhanced track will have the opportunity to test for Hebrew proﬁciency through the World Language Placement in Hebrew. For more information, contact hebrew-advise@umd.edu.
